other_observers: Andrew Spencer, Cole Wild; See above
specific_location: About 1/4 mile upstream from the Ismay Trading Post, not far downstream from where the private road crosses the stream
description: Birds were slender warblers, with short thin dark pointed bills and plumage that was pretty much entirely pearly gray, without yellowish tones. I got an excellent look at the reddish rump of one of the birds while it was preening, facing away from me in the tree.Behaviors: The birds were foraging actively in the lower deciduous vegetation along the watercourse. They appeared to be paired, as one apparently begged food from the other; it may have received a feeding, but I couldn't confirm this as the birds were behind a branch at the most critical moment--behavior was consistent with a feeding, however.Call: Birds were calling frequently, giving a high-pitched thin sharp unmusical "chip".Plumage: alternate
